caveats: a: BLS measures occupations across the economy and the broader 5415 family rather than this narrowed six-digit lens.
a: The evidence establishes automatable task types, not the wage-weighted share within eligible firms.
rho: No representative implementation-rate survey was found for the narrowed 541519 population.
rho: Implementation will differ sharply by client data access, tooling standardization, and regulated-customer mix.
e: No current public product-line split isolates the recurring-service share of six-digit 541519 LMM firms.
e: The injected firm count is not re-estimated here and may itself include business models outside the narrowed lens.
s5: The marketplace category combines IT and software services, is not NAICS 541519, and its reported 2025 median cash flow of 309062 dollars is generally below the stated LMM EBITDA band.
s5: Observed listings and completed marketplace transactions are not a representative census of control transfers.
q: The NAICS definition does not measure pricing or contract terms.
q: Provider mix between fixed-fee managed services and billable project work is unobserved for this lens.
d5: The source is a five-digit parent industry projection, not six-digit 541519.
d5: Employment growth can reflect wage, skill, or productivity changes rather than constant-price, constant-quality demand volume.
o: BLS occupation evidence does not measure the residual operator requirement for the narrowed industry.
o: Software vendor bundling and remote-management automation could displace a larger share than assumed.

envelope.interval_scope: research-input sensitivity with dataset anchors (l, n) held fixed

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: External business customers, principally through recurring support or recovery arrangements and repeat lifecycle work; fixed-fee, retainer, managed-service, and time-and-materials billing may coexist.
lens.deviation_from_default: Narrowed for coherence because the residual code explicitly combines disaster recovery and software installation with other computer-related services; the screen retains recurring or repeat outsourced operating services and omits one-off work.
lens.excluded_activities: One-off project-only installation with no repeat service, software publishing, custom programming, systems-integration design, facilities management, internal IT units, shells, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.included_activities: Lower-middle-market providers of recurring or repeat outsourced computer support, disaster-recovery, endpoint or software lifecycle installation, and related operational computer services sold to external customers.

caveats: BLS measures occupations across the economy and the broader 5415 family rather than this narrowed six-digit lens.
The evidence establishes automatable task types, not the wage-weighted share within eligible firms.
a.change_evidence: A payroll-weighted occupational census for sampled 541519 firms, ticket-system task logs, and a before-and-after study of generative-AI use by managed-service and disaster-recovery providers would materially change the estimate.
a.rationale: The retained work includes ticket intake, problem description capture, knowledge retrieval, routine troubleshooting, documentation, and user guidance. BLS identifies these duties and expects chatbots to reduce user-support employment, while fault diagnosis, onsite work, client communication, exception handling, and recovery accountability remain labor-intensive. The base is a wage-weighted judgment for current tasks not already automated.

d5.bridge: S3 measures 2024 to 2034 wage-and-salary employment in NAICS 541500, Computer Systems Design and Related Services, not constant-price demand in narrowed six-digit 541519. Its 15.8 percent ten-year projection is transformed to a five-year ratio by applying a constant compound rate, giving the 1.08 base. Because 541500 includes custom programming, systems design, and facilities management that are excluded from 541519, this transformation is more likely to overstate than understate demand growth for the retained service basket.
caveats: The source is a five-digit parent industry projection, not six-digit 541519.
Employment growth can reflect wage, skill, or productivity changes rather than constant-price, constant-quality demand volume.
d5.change_evidence: A current NAICS 541519 revenue series deflated with an appropriate services price index, customer IT-outsourcing spend forecasts separated by disaster recovery and lifecycle services, and renewal-volume data from providers would materially change the estimate.
d5.rationale: BLS projects 15.8 percent employment growth for the broader 541500 industry over 2024 to 2034 and attributes demand to IT infrastructure, software, cybersecurity, cloud computing, and AI-based systems. The base carries that broader forward signal into a shorter horizon with a conservative uncertainty band, while recognizing that employment is not constant-quality service quantity.

caveats: No current public product-line split isolates the recurring-service share of six-digit 541519 LMM firms.
The injected firm count is not re-estimated here and may itself include business models outside the narrowed lens.
e.change_evidence: A firm-level NAICS verification and revenue-by-service-line survey, supplemented by customer-contract samples from LMM firms, would materially change the estimate.
e.rationale: The official definition places computer disaster recovery and software installation in 541519 but leaves the code residual and broad. The base judgment removes LMM firms whose work is principally one-off installation or otherwise lacks a recurring or repeat outsourced service, retaining the operational-service portion.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541519 ($19,543,540,823) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541519 ($42,029,844,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $279,051 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541519); class <5: 8,483 firms, avg revenue/firm $429,327 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $67,190 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 841 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,781,555 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $278,813 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 505 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,678,558 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $575,694 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 526 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,815,287 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,692,592 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 179 firms, avg revenue/firm $40,627,151 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,358,149 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 116 firms, avg revenue/firm $190,265,517 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $29,776,553 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 705

caveats: BLS occupation evidence does not measure the residual operator requirement for the narrowed industry.
Software vendor bundling and remote-management automation could displace a larger share than assumed.
o.change_evidence: Customer ticket-resolution data by complexity, managed-service contract renewal data, and interviews with IT buyers on willingness to use self-service or vendor-native support would materially change the estimate.
o.rationale: BLS expects automated troubleshooting tools to reduce some user-support employment, but it also describes network maintenance, complex troubleshooting, installations, and disaster-recovery work that require accountable service delivery. The base assumes a substantial portion of future quantity still needs an operator, while routine queries and some setup are self-served or software-supplied.

caveats: The NAICS definition does not measure pricing or contract terms.
Provider mix between fixed-fee managed services and billable project work is unobserved for this lens.
q.change_evidence: Customer-contract samples showing billing basis, renewal clauses, service-level commitments, and realized price changes after AI deployment would materially change the estimate.
q.rationale: Recurring support and recovery arrangements can preserve some workflow savings inside a fixed fee or retainer, but hourly and time-and-materials work passes labor savings through promptly. Renewal repricing, capable competitors, standardized remote tools, and customer procurement reduce durable capture. The base retains less than half of implemented gross benefit.

caveats: No representative implementation-rate survey was found for the narrowed 541519 population.
Implementation will differ sharply by client data access, tooling standardization, and regulated-customer mix.
rho.change_evidence: Vendor-neutral benchmarks on AI deployment in managed-service providers, audited ticket-resolution quality data, and customer security questionnaires covering AI use would materially change the estimate.
rho.rationale: Knowledge-based intake and documentation can be implemented in standard ticket workflows, but production remediation, incident response, security access, customer trust, and quality assurance constrain rollout. The base assumes most routine support workflows can be operationalized during the horizon, with complex technical judgment retained.

caveats: The marketplace category combines IT and software services, is not NAICS 541519, and its reported 2025 median cash flow of 309062 dollars is generally below the stated LMM EBITDA band.
Observed listings and completed marketplace transactions are not a representative census of control transfers.
s5.change_evidence: A longitudinal ownership file for 541519, broker and quality-of-earnings transaction databases filtered to qualifying control transfers, and owner-age or succession survey responses would materially change the estimate.
s5.rationale: BizBuySell reported 44 completed IT and software service transactions in its 2025 data, demonstrating observed small-business transfer activity but not a denominator suitable for this lens. The base is a bounded five-year transfer judgment for eligible firms, allowing for owner succession, brokered sales, and strategic acquisitions while excluding closures and reorganizations.

principal_driver: AI can compress routine support, documentation, and knowledge-work effort while recurring outsourced technical responsibilities remain valuable.
principal_weakness: The residual NAICS mix and exposure of routine support to chatbots make both lens fit and long-run operator need uncertain.
